NKJV.1: Therefore, laying aside all malice, all deceit, hypocrisy, envy, and all evil speaking,
NKJV.2: as newborn babes, desire the pure milk of the word, that you may grow thereby,
NKJV.3: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is gracious.
NKJV.4: Coming to Him as to a living stone, rejected indeed by men, but chosen by God and precious,
NKJV.5: you also, as living stones, are being built up a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
NKJV.6: Therefore it is also contained in the Scripture,  “Behold, I lay in Zion A chief cornerstone, elect, precious, And he who believes on Him will by no means be put to shame.”
NKJV.7: Therefore, to you who believe, He is precious; but to those who are disobedient, “The stone which the builders rejected Has become the chief cornerstone,”
NKJV.8: and  “A stone of stumbling And a rock of offense.”   They stumble, being disobedient to the word, to which they also were appointed.
NKJV.9: But you are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, His own special people, that you may proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of darkness into His marvelous light;
NKJV.10: who once were not a people but are now the people of God, who had not obtained mercy but now have obtained mercy.
NKJV.11: Beloved, I beg you as sojourners and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts which war against the soul,
NKJV.12: having your conduct honorable among the Gentiles, that when they speak against you as evildoers, they may, by your good works which they observe, glorify God in the day of visitation.
NKJV.13: Therefore submit yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake, whether to the king as supreme,
NKJV.14: or to governors, as to those who are sent by him for the punishment of evildoers and for the praise of those who do good.
NKJV.15: For this is the will of God, that by doing good you may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men—
NKJV.16: as free, yet not using liberty as a cloak for vice, but as bondservants of God.
NKJV.17: Honor all people. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.
NKJV.18: Servants, be submissive to your masters with all fear, not only to the good and gentle, but also to the harsh.
NKJV.19: For this is commendable, if because of conscience toward God one endures grief, suffering wrongfully.
NKJV.20: For what credit is it if, when you are beaten for your faults, you take it patiently? But when you do good and suffer, if you take it patiently, this is commendable before God.
NKJV.21: For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving us an example, that you should follow His steps:
NKJV.22: “Who committed no sin, Nor was deceit found in His mouth”;
NKJV.23: who, when He was reviled, did not revile in return; when He suffered, He did not threaten, but committed Himself to Him who judges righteously;
NKJV.24: who Himself bore our sins in His own body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live for righteousness— by whose stripes you were healed.
NKJV.25: For you were like sheep going astray, but have now returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

ESV.1: So put away all malice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all slander.
ESV.2: Like newborn infants, long for the pure spiritual milk, that by it you may grow up into salvation —
ESV.3: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is good.
ESV.4: As you come to him, a living stone rejected by men but in the sight of God chosen and precious,
ESV.5: you yourselves like living stones are being built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
ESV.6: For it stands in Scripture: “Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone, a cornerstone chosen and precious, and whoever believes in him will not be put to shame.”
ESV.7: So the honor is for you who believe, but for those who do not believe, “The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone,”
ESV.8: and “A stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense.”  They stumble because they disobey the word, as they were destined to do.
ESV.9: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for his own possession, that you may proclaim the excellencies of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
ESV.10: Once you were not a people, but now you are God's people; once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
ESV.11: Beloved, I urge you as sojourners and exiles to abstain from the passions of the flesh, which wage war against your soul.
ESV.12: Keep your conduct among the Gentiles honorable, so that when they speak against you as evildoers, they may see your good deeds and glorify God on the day of visitation.
ESV.13: Be subject for the Lord's sake to every human institution,  whether it be to the emperor  as supreme,
ESV.14: or to governors as sent by him to punish those who do evil and to praise those who do good.
ESV.15: For this is the will of God, that by doing good you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish people.
ESV.16: Live as people who are free, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil, but living as servants  of God.
ESV.17: Honor everyone. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the emperor.
ESV.18: Servants, be subject to your masters with all respect, not only to the good and gentle but also to the unjust.
ESV.19: For this is a gracious thing, when, mindful of God, one endures sorrows while suffering unjustly.
ESV.20: For what credit is it if, when you sin and are beaten for it, you endure? But if when you do good and suffer for it you endure, this is a gracious thing in the sight of God.
ESV.21: For to this you have been called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you might follow in his steps.
ESV.22: He committed no sin, neither was deceit found in his mouth.
ESV.23: When he was reviled, he did not revile in return; when he suffered, he did not threaten, but continued entrusting himself to him who judges justly.
ESV.24: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. By his wounds you have been healed.
ESV.25: For you were straying like sheep, but have now returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

NASB.1: Therefore, putting aside all malice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all slander,
NASB.2: like newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the word, so that by it you may grow in respect to salvation,
NASB.3: if you have tasted the kindness of the Lord.
NASB.4: And coming to Him as to a living stone which has been rejected by men, but is choice and precious in the sight of God,
NASB.5: you also, as living stones, are being built up as a spiritual house for a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
NASB.6: For [this] is contained in Scripture: 'BEHOLD, I LAY IN ZION A CHOICE STONE, A PRECIOUS CORNER [stone], AND HE WHO BELIEVES IN HIM WILL NOT BE DISAPPOINTED.'
NASB.7: This precious value, then, is for you who believe; but for those who disbelieve, 'THE STONE WHICH THE BUILDERS REJECTED, THIS BECAME THE VERY CORNER [stone],'
NASB.8: and, 'A STONE OF STUMBLING AND A ROCK OF OFFENSE'; for they stumble because they are disobedient to the word, and to this [doom] they were also appointed.
NASB.9: But you are A CHOSEN RACE, A royal PRIESTHOOD, A HOLY NATION, A PEOPLE FOR [God's] OWN POSSESSION, so that you may proclaim the excellencies of Him who has called you out of darkness into His marvelous light;
NASB.10: for you once were NOT A PEOPLE, but now you are THE PEOPLE OF GOD; you had NOT RECEIVED MERCY, but now you have RECEIVED MERCY.
NASB.11: Beloved, I urge you as aliens and strangers to abstain from fleshly lusts which wage war against the soul.
NASB.12: Keep your behavior excellent among the Gentiles, so that in the thing in which they slander you as evildoers, they may because of your good deeds, as they observe [them], glorify God in the day of visitation.
NASB.13: Submit yourselves for the Lord's sake to every human institution, whether to a king as the one in authority,
NASB.14: or to governors as sent by him for the punishment of evildoers and the praise of those who do right.
NASB.15: For such is the will of God that by doing right you may silence the ignorance of foolish men.
NASB.16: [Act] as free men, and do not use your freedom as a covering for evil, but [use it] as bondslaves of God.
NASB.17: Honor all people, love the brotherhood, fear God, honor the king.
NASB.18: Servants, be submissive to your masters with all respect, not only to those who are good and gentle, but also to those who are unreasonable.
NASB.19: For this [finds] favor, if for the sake of conscience toward God a person bears up under sorrows when suffering unjustly.
NASB.20: For what credit is there if, when you sin and are harshly treated, you endure it with patience? But if when you do what is right and suffer [for it] you patiently endure it, this [finds] favor with God.
NASB.21: For you have been called for this purpose, since Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example for you to follow in His steps,
NASB.22: WHO COMMITTED NO SIN, NOR WAS ANY DECEIT FOUND IN HIS MOUTH;
NASB.23: and while being reviled, He did not revile in return; while suffering, He uttered no threats, but kept entrusting [Himself] to Him who judges righteously;
NASB.24: and He Himself bore our sins in His body on the cross, so that we might die to sin and live to righteousness; for by His wounds you were healed.
NASB.25: For you were continually straying like sheep, but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Guardian of your souls.

NLT.1: So get rid of all evil behavior. Be done with all deceit, hypocrisy, jealousy, and all unkind speech.
NLT.2: Like newborn babies, you must crave pure spiritual milk so that you will grow into a full experience of salvation. Cry out for this nourishment,
NLT.3: now that you have had a taste of the Lord’s kindness.
NLT.4: You are coming to Christ, who is the living cornerstone of God’s temple. He was rejected by people, but he was chosen by God for great honor.
NLT.5: And you are living stones that God is building into his spiritual temple. What’s more, you are his holy priests. Through the mediation of Jesus Christ, you offer spiritual sacrifices that please God.
NLT.6:
As the Scriptures say, “I am placing a cornerstone in Jerusalem,
chosen for great honor,
and anyone who trusts in him
will never be disgraced.”
NLT.7:
Yes, you who trust him recognize the honor God has given him. But for those who reject him, “The stone that the builders rejected
has now become the cornerstone.”
NLT.8:
And, “He is the stone that makes people stumble,
the rock that makes them fall.”
They stumble because they do not obey God’s word, and so they meet the fate that was planned for them.
NLT.9: But you are not like that, for you are a chosen people. You are royal priests, a holy nation, God’s very own possession. As a result, you can show others the goodness of God, for he called you out of the darkness into his wonderful light.
NLT.10:
“Once you had no identity as a people;
now you are God’s people.
Once you received no mercy;
now you have received God’s mercy.”
NLT.11: Dear friends, I warn you as “temporary residents and foreigners” to keep away from worldly desires that wage war against your very souls.
NLT.12: Be careful to live properly among your unbelieving neighbors. Then even if they accuse you of doing wrong, they will see your honorable behavior, and they will give honor to God when he judges the world.
NLT.13: For the Lord’s sake, submit to all human authority — whether the king as head of state,
NLT.14: or the officials he has appointed. For the king has sent them to punish those who do wrong and to honor those who do right.
NLT.15: It is God’s will that your honorable lives should silence those ignorant people who make foolish accusations against you.
NLT.16: For you are free, yet you are God’s slaves, so don’t use your freedom as an excuse to do evil.
NLT.17: Respect everyone, and love the family of believers. Fear God, and respect the king.
NLT.18: You who are slaves must submit to your masters with all respect. Do what they tell you — not only if they are kind and reasonable, but even if they are cruel.
NLT.19: For God is pleased when, conscious of his will, you patiently endure unjust treatment.
NLT.20: Of course, you get no credit for being patient if you are beaten for doing wrong. But if you suffer for doing good and endure it patiently, God is pleased with you.
NLT.21: For God called you to do good, even if it means suffering, just as Christ suffered for you. He is your example, and you must follow in his steps.
NLT.22:
He never sinned,
nor ever deceived anyone.
NLT.23:
He did not retaliate when he was insulted,
nor threaten revenge when he suffered.
He left his case in the hands of God,
who always judges fairly.
NLT.24:
He personally carried our sins
in his body on the cross
so that we can be dead to sin
and live for what is right.
By his wounds
you are healed.
NLT.25:
Once you were like sheep
who wandered away.
But now you have turned to your Shepherd,
the Guardian of your souls.

WEB.1: Putting away therefore all wickedness, all deceit, hypocrisies, envies, and all evil speaking,
WEB.2: as newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the Word, that with it you may grow,
WEB.3: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is gracious:
WEB.4: coming to him, a living stone, rejected indeed by men, but chosen by God, precious.
WEB.5: You also, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
WEB.6: Because it is contained in Scripture, “Behold,  I lay in Zion a chief cornerstone, chosen, and precious: He who believes in him will not be disappointed.”
WEB.7: For you who believe therefore is the honor, but for those who are disobedient, “The stone which the builders rejected, has become the chief cornerstone,”
WEB.8: and, “a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense.”   For they stumble at the word, being disobedient, to which also they were appointed.
WEB.9: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God’s own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light:
WEB.10: who in time past were no people, but now are God’s people, who had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.
WEB.11: Beloved, I beg you as foreigners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;
WEB.12: having good behavior among the nations, so in that of which they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good works, which they see, glorify God in the day of visitation.
WEB.13: Therefore subject yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether to the king, as supreme;
WEB.14: or to governors, as sent by him for vengeance on evildoers and for praise to those who do well.
WEB.15: For this is the will of God, that by well-doing you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:
WEB.16: as free, and not using your freedom for a cloak of wickedness, but as bondservants of God.
WEB.17: Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.
WEB.18: Servants, be in subjection to your masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the wicked.
WEB.19: For it is commendable if someone endures pain, suffering unjustly, because of conscience toward God.
WEB.20: For what glory is it if, when you sin, you patiently endure beating? But if, when you do well, you patiently endure suffering, this is commendable with God.
WEB.21: For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving you  an example, that you should follow his steps,
WEB.22: who did not sin, “neither was deceit found in his mouth.”
WEB.23: Who, when he was cursed, didn’t curse back. When he suffered, didn’t threaten, but committed himself to him who judges righteously;
WEB.24: who his own self bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness; by whose stripes you were healed.
WEB.25: For you were going astray like sheep; but now have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer  of your souls.

CSB17.1: Therefore, rid yourselves of <sup>ⓛ</sup> all malice, all deceit, hypocrisy, envy, and all slander. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.2: Like newborn infants, desire the pure milk of the word, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> so that you may grow up into your salvation, <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.3: if you have tasted ⓐ that the Lord is good. [3] ⓑ
CSB17.4: As you come to him, a living stone — rejected by people but chosen and honored by <sup>[4]</sup> God —
CSB17.5: you yourselves, as living stones, a spiritual house, are being built to be a holy priesthood <sup>[5]</sup> to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God <sup>ⓒ</sup> through Jesus Christ.
CSB17.6:
For it stands in Scripture: See, I lay a stone in Zion, ⓓ
a chosen and honored [6] cornerstone, ⓔ
and the one who believes in him
will never be put to shame. [7] ⓕ
CSB17.7:
So honor will come to you who believe; but for the unbelieving, The stone that the builders rejected —
this one has become the cornerstone, [8] ⓖ
CSB17.8:
and A stone to stumble over,
and a rock to trip over. [9] ⓗ
They stumble because they disobey the word; they were destined ⓘ for this.
CSB17.9: But you are a chosen race, [10] [11] [12] a royal priesthood, [13] ⓙ a holy nation, [14] ⓚ a people for his possession, [15] ⓛ so that you may proclaim the praises [16] [17] ⓜ of the one who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light. ⓝ
CSB17.10: Once you were not a people, but now you are God’s people; you had not received mercy, <sup>ⓐ</sup> but now you have received mercy.
CSB17.11: Dear friends, I urge you as strangers and exiles <sup>ⓑ</sup> to abstain <sup>ⓒ</sup> from sinful <sup>ⓓ</sup> desires that wage war against the soul. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.12: Conduct <sup>ⓕ</sup> yourselves honorably among the Gentiles, <sup>[18]</sup> so that when they slander you as evildoers, they will observe your good works and will glorify God on the day he visits. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.13: Submit <sup>ⓗ</sup> to every human authority because of the Lord, whether to the emperor <sup>[19]</sup> as the supreme authority <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.14: or to governors as those sent out by him to punish those who do what is evil and to praise those who do what is good.
CSB17.15: For it is God’s will <sup>ⓙ</sup> that you silence the ignorance <sup>ⓚ</sup> of foolish people by doing good.
CSB17.16: Submit as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil, <sup>ⓛ</sup> but as God’s slaves. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.17: Honor everyone. Love <sup>ⓝ</sup> the brothers and sisters. <sup>ⓐ</sup> Fear God. <sup>ⓑ</sup> Honor the emperor.
CSB17.18: Household slaves, submit to your masters <sup>ⓒ</sup> with all reverence <sup>ⓓ</sup> not only to the good and gentle ones but also to the cruel. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.19: For it brings favor if, because of a consciousness of God, someone endures grief from suffering unjustly.
CSB17.20: For what credit is there if when you do wrong and are beaten, you endure it? But when you do what is good and suffer, <sup>ⓕ</sup> if you endure it, this brings favor with God.
CSB17.21: For you were called to this, because Christ also suffered <sup>ⓖ</sup> for you, leaving you an example, <sup>ⓗ</sup> that you should follow <sup>ⓘ</sup> in his steps.
CSB17.22: He did not commit sin, ⓙ and no deceit ⓚ was found in his mouth; [20] ⓛ
CSB17.23: when he was insulted, he did not insult in return; when he suffered, he did not threaten but entrusted himself to the one who judges <sup>ⓜ</sup> justly.
CSB17.24: He himself bore our sins ⓝ in his body ⓐ on the tree; ⓑ so that, having died to sins, ⓒ we might live for righteousness. ⓓ By his wounds [21] ⓔ you have been healed.
CSB17.25: For you were like sheep going astray, [22] ⓕ but you have now returned to the Shepherd ⓖ and Overseer [23] of your souls.

AMP.1: So put aside every trace of malice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all slander and hateful speech;
AMP.2: like newborn babies [you should] long for the pure milk of the word, so that by it you may be nurtured and grow in respect to salvation [its ultimate fulfillment],
AMP.3: if in fact you have [already] tasted the goodness and gracious kindness of the Lord.
AMP.4: Come to Him [the risen Lord] as to a living Stone which men rejected and threw away, but which is choice and precious in the sight of God.
AMP.5: You [believers], like living stones, are being built up into a spiritual house for a holy and dedicated priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices [that are] acceptable and pleasing to God through Jesus Christ.
AMP.6:
For this is contained in Scripture:
"Behold, I am laying in Zion a chosen stone, a precious (honored) Cornerstone,
And he who believes in Him [whoever adheres to, trusts in, and relies on Him] will never be disappointed [in his expectations]."
AMP.7:
This precious value, then, is for you who believe [in Him as God's only Son—the Source of salvation]; but for those who disbelieve,
"The [very] stone which the builders rejected
Has become the chief Cornerstone,"
AMP.8:
and,
"A stone of stumbling and a rock of offense";
for they stumble because they disobey the word [of God], and to this they [who reject Him as Savior] were also appointed.
AMP.9: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a consecrated nation, a [special] people for God's own possession, so that you may proclaim the excellencies [the wonderful deeds and virtues and perfections] of Him who called you out of darkness into His marvelous light.
AMP.10: Once you were not a people [at all], but now you are God's people; once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
AMP.11: Beloved, I urge you as aliens and strangers [in this world] to abstain from the sensual urges [those dishonorable desires] that wage war against the soul.
AMP.12: Keep your behavior excellent among the [unsaved] Gentiles [conduct yourself honorably, with graciousness and integrity], so that for whatever reason they may slander you as evildoers, yet by observing your good deeds they may [instead come to] glorify God in the day of visitation [when He looks upon them with mercy].
AMP.13: Submit yourselves to [the authority of] every human institution for the sake of the Lord [to honor His name], whether it is to a king as one in a position of power,
AMP.14: or to governors as sent by him to bring punishment to those who do wrong, and to praise and encourage those who do right.
AMP.15: For it is the will of God that by doing right you may silence (muzzle, gag) the [culpable] ignorance and irresponsible criticisms of foolish people.
AMP.16: Live as free people, but do not use your freedom as a cover or pretext for evil, but [use it and live] as bond-servants of God.
AMP.17: Show respect for all people [treat them honorably], love the brotherhood [of believers], fear God, honor the king.
AMP.18: Servants, be submissive to your masters with all [proper] respect, not only to those who are good and kind, but also to those who are unreasonable.
AMP.19: For this finds favor, if a person endures the sorrow of suffering unjustly because of an awareness of [the will of] God.
AMP.20: After all, what kind of credit is there if, when you do wrong and are punished for it, you endure it patiently? But if when you do what is right and patiently bear [undeserved] suffering, this finds favor with God.
AMP.21: For [as a believer] you have been called for this purpose, since Christ suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you may follow in His footsteps.
AMP.22: <e>He committed no sin, nor was deceit ever found in His mouth</e>.
AMP.23: While being reviled and insulted, He did not revile or insult in return; while suffering, He made no threats [of vengeance], but kept entrusting Himself to Him who judges fairly.
AMP.24: He personally carried our sins in His body on the cross [willingly offering Himself on it, as on an altar of sacrifice], so that we might die to sin [becoming immune from the penalty and power of sin] and live for righteousness; for by His wounds you [who believe] have been healed.
AMP.25: For you were continually wandering like [so many] sheep, but now you have come back to the Shepherd and Guardian of your souls.

MSG.1: So clean house! Make a clean sweep of malice and pretense, envy and hurtful talk.
MSG.2: You've had a taste of God.
MSG.3: Now, like infants at the breast, drink deep of God's pure kindness. Then you'll grow up mature and whole in God.
MSG.4: Welcome to the living Stone, the source of life. The workmen took one look and threw it out; God set it in the place of honor.
MSG.5: Present yourselves as building stones for the construction of a sanctuary vibrant with life, in which you'll serve as holy priests offering Christ-approved lives up to God.
MSG.6: The Scriptures provide precedent: Look! I'm setting a stone in Zion, a cornerstone in the place of honor. Whoever trusts in this stone as a foundation will never have cause to regret it.
MSG.7: To you who trust him, he's a Stone to be proud of, but to those who refuse to trust him, The stone the workmen threw out is now the chief foundation stone.
MSG.8: For the untrusting it's ... a stone to trip over, a boulder blocking the way. They trip and fall because they refuse to obey, just as predicted.
MSG.9: But you are the ones chosen by God, chosen for the high calling of priestly work, chosen to be a holy people, God's instruments to do his work and speak out for him, to tell others of the night-and-day difference he made for you—
MSG.10: from nothing to something, from rejected to accepted.
MSG.11: Friends, this world is not your home, so don't make yourselves cozy in it. Don't indulge your ego at the expense of your soul.
MSG.12: Live an exemplary life among the natives so that your actions will refute their prejudices. Then they'll be won over to God's side and be there to join in the celebration when he arrives.
MSG.13: Make the Master proud of you by being good citizens. Respect the authorities, whatever their level;
MSG.14: they are God's emissaries for keeping order.
MSG.15: It is God's will that by doing good, you might cure the ignorance of the fools who think you're a danger to society.
MSG.16: Exercise your freedom by serving God, not by breaking the rules.
MSG.17: Treat everyone you meet with dignity. Love your spiritual family. Revere God. Respect the government.
MSG.18: You who are servants, be good servants to your masters—not just to good masters, but also to bad ones.
MSG.19: What counts is that you put up with it for God's sake when you're treated badly for no good reason.
MSG.20: There's no particular virtue in accepting punishment that you well deserve. But if you're treated badly for good behavior and continue in spite of it to be a good servant, that is what counts with God.
MSG.21: This is the kind of life you've been invited into, the kind of life Christ lived. He suffered everything that came his way so you would know that it could be done, and also know how to do it, step-by-step.
MSG.22: He never did one thing wrong, Not once said anything amiss.
MSG.23: They called him every name in the book and he said nothing back. He suffered in silence, content to let God set things right.
MSG.24: He used his servant body to carry our sins to the Cross so we could be rid of sin, free to live the right way. His wounds became your healing.
MSG.25: You were lost sheep with no idea who you were or where you were going. Now you're named and kept for good by the Shepherd of your souls.

NIV.1: Therefore, rid yourselves of all malice and all deceit, hypocrisy, envy, and slander of every kind.
NIV.2: Like newborn babies, crave pure spiritual milk, so that by it you may grow up in your salvation,
NIV.3: now that you have tasted that the Lord is good.
NIV.4:
The Living Stone and a Chosen People
As you come to him, the living Stone—rejected by men but chosen by God and precious to him—
NIV.5: you also, like living stones, are being built into a spiritual house to be a holy priesthood, offering spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
NIV.6:
For in Scripture it says:
“See, I lay a stone in Zion,
a chosen and precious cornerstone,
and the one who trusts in him
will never be put to shame.”
NIV.7:
Now to you who believe, this stone is precious. But to those who do not believe,
“The stone the builders rejected
has become the capstone,”
NIV.8:
and,
“A stone that causes men to stumble
and a rock that makes them fall.”
They stumble because they disobey the message—which is also what they were destined for.
NIV.9: But you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people belonging to God, that you may declare the praises of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.
NIV.10: Once you were not a people, but now you are the people of God; once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
NIV.11: Dear friends, I urge you, as aliens and strangers in the world, to abstain from sinful desires, which war against your soul.
NIV.12: Live such good lives among the pagans that, though they accuse you of doing wrong, they may see your good deeds and glorify God on the day he visits us.
NIV.13:
Submission to Rulers and Masters
Submit yourselves for the Lord’s sake to every authority instituted among men: whether to the king, as the supreme authority,
NIV.14: or to governors, who are sent by him to punish those who do wrong and to commend those who do right.
NIV.15: For it is God’s will that by doing good you should silence the ignorant talk of foolish men.
NIV.16: Live as free men, but do not use your freedom as a cover-up for evil; live as servants of God.
NIV.17: Show proper respect to everyone: Love the brotherhood of believers, fear God, honor the king.
NIV.18: Slaves, submit yourselves to your masters with all respect, not only to those who are good and considerate, but also to those who are harsh.
NIV.19: For it is commendable if a man bears up under the pain of unjust suffering because he is conscious of God.
NIV.20: But how is it to your credit if you receive a beating for doing wrong and endure it? But if you suffer for doing good and you endure it, this is commendable before God.
NIV.21: To this you were called, because Christ suffered for you, leaving you an example, that you should follow in his steps.
NIV.22:
“He committed no sin,
and no deceit was found in his mouth.”
NIV.23: When they hurled their insults at him, he did not retaliate; when he suffered, he made no threats. Instead, he entrusted himself to him who judges justly.
NIV.24: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, so that we might die to sins and live for righteousness; by his wounds you have been healed.
NIV.25: For you were like sheep going astray, but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

NRSVCE.1: Rid yourselves, therefore, of all malice, and all guile, insincerity, envy, and all slander.
NRSVCE.2: Like newborn infants, long for the pure, spiritual milk, so that by it you may grow into salvation —
NRSVCE.3: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is good.
NRSVCE.4: Come to him, a living stone, though rejected by mortals yet chosen and precious in God’s sight, and
NRSVCE.5: like living stones, let yourselves be built into a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.6:
For it stands in scripture: “See, I am laying in Zion a stone,
a cornerstone chosen and precious;
and whoever believes in him will not be put to shame.”
NRSVCE.7:
To you then who believe, he is precious; but for those who do not believe, “The stone that the builders rejected
has become the very head of the corner,”
NRSVCE.8:
and “A stone that makes them stumble,
and a rock that makes them fall.”
They stumble because they disobey the word, as they were destined to do.
NRSVCE.9: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God’s own people, in order that you may proclaim the mighty acts of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
NRSVCE.10:
Once you were not a people,
but now you are God’s people;
once you had not received mercy,
but now you have received mercy.
NRSVCE.11: Beloved, I urge you as aliens and exiles to abstain from the desires of the flesh that wage war against the soul.
NRSVCE.12: Conduct yourselves honorably among the Gentiles, so that, though they malign you as evildoers, they may see your honorable deeds and glorify God when he comes to judge.
NRSVCE.13: For the Lord’s sake accept the authority of every human institution, whether of the emperor as supreme,
NRSVCE.14: or of governors, as sent by him to punish those who do wrong and to praise those who do right.
NRSVCE.15: For it is God’s will that by doing right you should silence the ignorance of the foolish.
NRSVCE.16: As servants of God, live as free people, yet do not use your freedom as a pretext for evil.
NRSVCE.17: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Fear God. Honor the emperor.
NRSVCE.18: Slaves, accept the authority of your masters with all deference, not only those who are kind and gentle but also those who are harsh.
NRSVCE.19: For it is a credit to you if, being aware of God, you endure pain while suffering unjustly.
NRSVCE.20: If you endure when you are beaten for doing wrong, what credit is that? But if you endure when you do right and suffer for it, you have God’s approval.
NRSVCE.21: For to this you have been called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you should follow in his steps.
NRSVCE.22:
“He committed no sin,
and no deceit was found in his mouth.”
NRSVCE.23: When he was abused, he did not return abuse; when he suffered, he did not threaten; but he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
NRSVCE.24: He himself bore our sins in his body on the cross, so that, free from sins, we might live for righteousness; by his wounds you have been healed.
NRSVCE.25: For you were going astray like sheep, but now you have returned to the shepherd and guardian of your souls.

NET.1: So get rid of all evil and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all slander.
NET.2: And yearn like newborn infants for pure, spiritual milk, so that by it you may grow up to salvation,
NET.3: if you have experienced the Lord’s kindness.
NET.4: So as you come to him, a living stone rejected by men but chosen and priceless in God’s sight,
NET.5: you yourselves, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house to be a holy priesthood and to offer spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
NET.6: For it says in scripture, “Look, I lay in Zion a stone, a chosen and priceless cornerstone, and whoever believes in him will never be put to shame.”
NET.7: So you who believe see his value, but for those who do not believe, the stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone,
NET.8: and a stumbling-stone and a rock to trip over. They stumble because they disobey the word, as they were destined to do.
NET.9: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people of his own, so that you may proclaim the virtues of the one who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
NET.10: You once were not a people, but now you are God’s people. You were shown no mercy, but now you have received mercy.
NET.11: Dear friends, I urge you as foreigners and exiles to keep away from fleshly desires that do battle against the soul,
NET.12: and maintain good conduct among the non-Christians, so that though they now malign you as wrongdoers, they may see your good deeds and glorify God when he appears.
NET.13: Be subject to every human institution for the Lord’s sake, whether to a king as supreme
NET.14: or to governors as those he commissions to punish wrongdoers and praise those who do good.
NET.15: For God wants you to silence the ignorance of foolish people by doing good.
NET.16: Live as free people, not using your freedom as a pretext for evil, but as God’s slaves.
NET.17: Honor all people, love the family of believers, fear God, honor the king.
NET.18: Slaves, be subject to your masters with all reverence, not only to those who are good and gentle, but also to those who are perverse.
NET.19: For this finds God’s favor, if because of conscience toward God someone endures hardships in suffering unjustly.
NET.20: For what credit is it if you sin and are mistreated and endure it? But if you do good and suffer and so endure, this finds favor with God.
NET.21: For to this you were called, since Christ also suffered for you, leaving an example for you to follow in his steps.
NET.22: He committed no sin nor was deceit found in his mouth.
NET.23: When he was maligned, he did not answer back; when he suffered, he threatened no retaliation, but committed himself to God who judges justly.
NET.24: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we may cease from sinning and live for righteousness. By his wounds you were healed.
NET.25: For you were going astray like sheep but now you have turned back to the shepherd and guardian of your souls.
